records.ety: [Cf. L. vomere, vomitum, and v. freq. vomitare. See Vomit, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Vomited; p. pr. & vb. n. Vomiting.]

defs: To eject the contents of the stomach by the mouth; to puke; to spew.
To throw up; to eject from the stomach through the mouth; to disgorge; to puke; to spew out; -- often followed by up or out.
quotes: The fish . . . vomited out Jonah upon the dry land.

defs: Hence, to eject from any hollow place; to belch forth; to emit; to throw forth; as, volcanoes vomit flame, stones, etc.
quotes: Like the sons of Vulcan, vomit smoke.

records.ety: [L. vomitus, from vomere, vomitum, to vomit; akin to Gr. <?/, Skr. vam, Lith. vemiti. Cf. Emetic, Vomito.]

defs: Matter that is vomited; esp., matter ejected from the stomach through the mouth.
quotes: Like vomit from his yawning entrails poured.

defs: That which excites vomiting; an emetic.
quotes: He gives your Hollander a vomit.
